natural_language: Alright, here's what we've got. Two separate stacks on the table. The left stack: from bottom to top it goes A, then B, then C, then D — four blocks high. The right stack is shorter: E on the bottom with F riding on top. So that's a pair of blocks. The top of each stack is exposed (D on the left, F on the right), so those are the only ones you can grab right now. The manipulator is empty. What we want: combine everything into a single six-block tower. Reading from floor to ceiling: A at the base, B next, then C, then D, then E, and finally F crowning the top. So we're threading the right stack onto the left stack, effectively.
